items.title: Risk of Jaslinda attacked by wild animals, fall is low, says mountain guide
items.summary: Jaslinda was last seen by a mountain guide about 7.30am on Sunday, May 24. Mountain guide Muzafar Mohamad said the likelihood of either incident was small, based on Jaslinda's previous experience climbing the mountain. The Perak Fire and Rescue Department said the additional rescuers comprise six members of the department's Special Tactical Operation and Rescue Team (Storm), two Department of Wildlife and National Parks (Perhilitan) protection team members, five Forestry Department personnel, and three forestry mountain guides.

items.title: 'Deaf' remark not aimed at Pas, Tun Faisal tells Ahmad Fadhli
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A Bersatu leader has questioned why Pas information chief Ahmad Fadhli Shaari reacted defensively to a "deaf" label used during a Bersatu ceramah in Melaka recently. Bersatu information chief Datuk Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz said he believed the remarks made by party secretary-general Datuk Seri Azmin Ali, who used the term "deaf" to describe those claiming the party lacks election machinery, were not directed at Pas. Tun Faisal was responding to Ahmad Fadhli, after the latter suggested it was time for Pas to move independently of Bersatu in future elections.

items.title: Man with 24 priors arrested over Cheras kopitiam gold chain-snatch theft
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have arrested a man linked to a gold chain-snatch theft in Taman Connaught last month. Cheras police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Rosdi Daud said the couple were seated outdoors when a man on a motorcycle approached from behind. Mohd Rosdi said the court rejected the police application to remand the suspect, who has since been released on police bail.

items.title: MetMalaysia issues evening thunderstorm and heavy rain warning for Pahang, Johor and Sabah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 31 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in three states until 7pm today. In a post on Facebook, MetMalaysia said that the bad weather is expected to hit Pahang, involving Jerantut, Temerloh, Maran, Kuantan, Bera, Pekan and Rompin. The same warning was also issued in Johor involving the Mersing area, while in Sabah, the area expected to be hit by the bad weather is Sandakan (Telupid and Beluran).

items.title: Optimax sees strong volume recovery despite higher costs, says HLIB
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Optimax Holdings Bhd has not experienced any significant increase in supply-related costs, including consumables, despite the ongoing Iran war at this juncture. According to Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB), the company has seen a strong rebound in business volume during the April–May period, with the momentum expected to continue into the second quarter of financial year 2026 (Q2 FY26). HLIB noted that Optimax posted a core profit after tax and minority interest (PATMI) of RM2.8 million in Q1 FY26, down 30.6 per cent quarter-on-quarter and 17.6 per cent year-on-year (YoY), which it deemed in line with both its own and consensus full-year forecasts at 16 per cent and 17 per cent, respectively.

items.title: Abang Johari: Sarawak must develop local expertise in aerospace, satellite technologies
items.summary: KUCHING, May 31 — Sarawak is laying the groundwork to develop local expertise in aerospace, satellite engineering, data analytics and future communication technologies ahead of its planned venture into the aerospace and satellite sectors. Sarawak Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg said the effort is crucial in line with the state’s plans to explore the aerospace and satellite technology sectors, which are expected to play a strategic role in future development. “Therefore, Sarawak must begin preparing now to develop local expertise in aerospace, satellite engineering, data analytics, and future communication technologies,” he said in his message in conjunction with the 2026 Gawai Dayak celebration today.

items.title: Search for missing hiker Jaslinda intensifies with additional manpower
items.summary: As of 10am, 21 rescue personnel remain in the Gunung Batu Putih search sector, 13 from the department, three from the Forestry Department, and five Perak Forestry Mountain Guides (MGP). Sayani said six firemen have reached the peak of Gunung Rayu. The SAR operation continues around Gunung Rayu with department and MGP personnel," said Sayani.

items.title: MBM Resources to benefit from strong Perodua demand, policy support
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: MBM Resources Bhd is poised to benefit from sustained demand for Perodua vehicles, backed by a healthy order backlog of about 43,000 units and continued strong new bookings. Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB) said supportive government measures, including the RM4,000 matching grant for scrapping vehicles aged over 20 years for purchases of Perodua and Proton models, are expected to further lift demand. HLIB said the recent launch of Perodua's new B-segment sport utility vehicle (SUV), the Traz, has received encouraging demand.

items.title: Israel troops capture Beaufort Castle in southern Lebanon push against Hizbollah
items.summary: TEL AVIV: Israeli troops have seized the 900-year-old Beaufort Castle and its strategic ridge in southern Lebanon, the military said on Sunday, a major advance against Iran-backed Hizbollah despite a ceasefire announced more than six weeks ago. One Israeli soldier was killed, the military said. Israeli troops were also operating near Nabatieh, a major Hizbollah stronghold in southern Lebanon, the military said.

items.title: Viral video of alleged fight near Kaamatan celebrations in Penampang prompts calls for investigation
items.summary: PENAMPANG, May 31 — A video circulating on social media allegedly showing a fight involving several individuals near the KDCA grounds during the Kaamatan celebrations has sparked widespread reactions online. The clip, which has been shared online, prompted calls from netizens for authorities to investigate the alleged incident and identify those involved. However, comments posted by social media users regarding the cause of the alleged altercation, the identities of the individuals involved, or whether alcohol was a factor remain unverified.

items.title: US castigates Europe over defence spend as Nato reassures Asia
items.summary: SINGAPORE: The United States fired a fresh broadside at its Nato allies in Singapore over the weekend but Western European officials insisted the grouping remains resilient. Speaking at the Shangri-La Dialogue, US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth praised Asian partners for boosting defence spending and aligning closely with Washington as tensions with China mount. "Europe and Nato have some big decisions to make," he said.

items.title: Sarawak’s future depends on STEM, TVET and cultural resilience, says Wan Junaidi in Gawai message
items.summary: KUCHING, May 31 — Greater emphasis must be placed on Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics (STEM) and Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) among the Bumiputera community in Sarawak to avoid widening economic disparities and improve their competitiveness in the future workforce, said Yang di-Pertua Negeri Tun Pehin Sri Dr Wan Junaidi Tuanku Jaafar. Although challenging, they will guarantee a brighter and more competitive future,” he said in his Gawai Dayak message. The Head of State said the Dayak community, together with other communities in Sarawak, has played a significant role in critical sectors including agriculture, plantations, the public service, private industry and professional fields.

items.title: Redefining our economic resilience in the face of Middle East crisis
items.summary: The crisis in the Middle East is a direct hit to Malaysia's supply lines, fiscal space, and industrial core. We often frame these conflicts through the narrow lens of "oil price hikes". Take shipping, for example.

items.title: 52 cases, 114 people: Missing hiker cases up nearly 30pct this year
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: There have been 52 recorded cases of hikers getting lost this year, involving 114 people. "Last year, a total of 88 missing cases involving 193 victims were reported, with Selangor recording the highest number of search and rescue (SAR) operations for lost hikers," he said. Nor Hisham said areas such as Bukit Kiara, Bukit Gasing and Bukit Sungai Besi recorded a high number of cases.

items.title: Explainer: Inside Malaysia's B15 move
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Effective June 1, Malaysia will advance its energy transition by raising the Peninsular biodiesel blend from B10 to B15, a move aimed at easing dependence on imported diesel while giving a bigger role to palm oil in the country's fuel mix. The rest remains conventional petroleum diesel. For Malaysia's palm oil industry, the shift translates into a meaningful lift in domestic demand.

items.title: Can strategic partnerships still be trusted? Khaled Nordin questions international agreements after Norway missile cancellation
items.summary: SINGAPORE, May 31 — Malaysia has raised concerns over what it described as the growing practice of double standards in the enforcement of international law, said Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin. Speaking at the sixth plenary session of the 23rd IISS Shangri-La Dialogue 2026 today, the minister said violations of the international law are frequently met with inconsistent and selective reactions depending on those involved. He said international law is often treated as something that exists only on paper, observed strictly by countries of the global majority, but selectively interpreted by stronger powers whenever convenient.

items.title: Bayan Lepas road rage: Drug-positive Myvi driver's remand extended
items.summary: BALIK PULAU: Police have obtained a two-day remand extension for a 37-year-old man following a road rage incident in which he allegedly criminally intimidated another driver and made obscene gestures along Jalan Tun Dr Awang on Thursday. Southwest district police chief Assistant Commissioner Anuwal Ab Wahab said the remand extension began today. Police subsequently arrested the suspect following investigations into the incident.

items.title: China patrols Scarborough Shoal after Philippines warns of threat
items.summary: BEIJING: China's military and coast guard said they carried out patrols near disputed waters in the South China Sea on Sunday, a day after the Philippines said it remained under threat from Beijing despite a recent easing in US-China tensions. Philippine and US forces held a five-day maritime exercise in the same waters last week near the Scarborough Shoal, the third such drill this year, to strengthen interoperability and maritime security, the Philippine military said on Sunday. Taiwan makes broadly similar claims to China's in the South China Sea.

items.title: MR DIY seen weathering Middle East tensions, conrtinues expansion
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The impact of escalating tensions in the Middle East on MR DIY Group (M) Bhd has remained manageable so far, supported by the group's strong balance sheet, bargaining power and a firmer ringgit, according to RHB Research. RHB Research analyst Soong Wei Siang said the retailer has been able to optimise unit costs despite global cost pressures, leveraging its scale and procurement strength amid geopolitical uncertainties. On external risks, Soong said MR DIY has so far experienced a manageable impact from ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, particularly in terms of cost pressures.

items.title: Producer says decision to halt ‘Konspirasi’ screenings made internally, no outside influence
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 31 — Production company Elepfilm Entertainment Sdn Bhd has emphasised that the decision to halt screenings of the film “Konspirasi” in cinemas nationwide was made internally by its management without influence from external parties. No party influenced the decision,” it said. The statement was made following various speculations about the termination of the film’s screenings since May 28.

items.title: Who’s joining Bersama? Nik Nazmi reveals breakdown of party’s first 20,000 members
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 31 — Nearly half of the first 20,000 people to join Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) had never belonged to any political party before, former Setiawangsa MP Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad said today. Former PKR members made up 29 per cent of Bersama’s first 20,000 sign-ups. Another 6.3 per cent came from Umno, followed by PAS at 5.2 per cent.

items.title: Tuan Ibrahim denies failing to pay service centre rent
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pas deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man has dismissed allegations suggesting that he failed to pay rent for his Kubang Kerian constituency service centre in Kelantan. Tuan Ibrahim said he had diligently settled his office's monthly rent from January last year until April this year. However, the rental payment for May was deducted from his security deposit following an eviction notice that allegedly violated the tenancy agreement.

items.title: Police rule Myanmar worker’s death in Penang entertainment outlet fire as sudden death
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 31 — Police have classified the death of a Myanmar man found in a fire at an entertainment premises on Jalan Penang last night as sudden death. Northeast District deputy police chief Supt Lee Swee Sake advised the public against speculating on the incident as it could affect police investigations into the cause of death and the Malaysian Fire and Rescue Department’s (JBPM) probe into the cause of the fire. He said police received information about the incident from members of the public at 8.50pm, before firefighters later found the body of the 56-year-old victim.

items.title: Melaka hotel apologises, offers full refunds after system glitch triggers check-in chaos
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A hotel in Melaka has apologised to guests affected by a severe check-in disruption over the weekend. In a statement posted on its Facebook page, Birkin International Hotel said it had offered refunds and compensation to all affected guests. Hotel finance director Jac Khoo told the New Straits Times that the operational chaos was triggered by a technical failure within a third-party and wholesale booking system, rather than the hotel's internal system.

items.title: Europe's green jet fuels see upside in Iran war
items.summary: Interest in such synthetic propellants is growing as the Iran war pushes Europe to reassess its dependencies, raising hopes of a turnaround for the struggling sector, according to industry experts. Before the war squeezed Europe's jet fuel supplies, electro-Sustainable Aviation Fuel (e-SAF), was largely touted for its climate friendly credentials. Germany's armed forces recently tested Ineratec's fuel.

items.title: 'Too many flies,' say villagers enduring long-standing infestation
items.summary: TAMPIN: Since last year, villagers of Kampung Ulu Air Kuning Selatan have been enduring a persistent fly infestation that has disrupted daily life, with many blaming a nearby chicken farm. They said the situation has made daily life increasingly difficult, as flies invade homes in large numbers and disrupt basic routines, while also raising concerns over possible health risks. Nazaruddin Johari, 49, claimed that the problem is believed to be caused by a chicken farm near the village.

items.title: 100pct redemption rate: Teachers top up own money to maximise Madani book vouchers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The 2025 Madani teachers' book voucher programme has fostered stronger reading habits among educators nationwide, a study has revealed. She said 428,027 teachers redeemed the vouchers, amounting to RM42.8 million, with a 100 per cent redemption rate and a 99.74 per cent book purchase rate. "63.2 per cent said they used the vouchers specifically to enhance their professional knowledge, with 49 per cent purchasing books related to professional development," she said when presenting findings at the Kuala Lumpur International Book Fair today.

items.title: Indo-Pacific nations deepen defence ties as US doubts grow and China ascends
items.summary: At Asia's premier defence forum on Saturday, US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth pressed regional partners to shoulder more of the security burden. "All the defence secretaries here present are unanimous in the need to make agile and speedy upscaling of their own individual defence capabilities," Philippines' Defence Secretary Gilberto Teodoro told Reuters. He described it as "buttressing" the US's traditional role, with Manila deepening defence ties with partners such as Japan, Australia, Canada and New Zealand.

items.title: Police seek public help to find missing 16-year-old girl in Temerloh
items.summary: KUANTAN: Police are seeking public assistance to locate a 16-year-old girl who has been missing since May 14 in Temerloh. In a statement today, acting Jerantut district police chief Deputy Superintendent Arshad Ayob said a missing person report on Ainul Maisarah Abu Bakar was lodged by her father about 10am yesterday. Members of the public with information are urged to contact the Jerantut district police headquarters' District Control Centre at 09-2662222 or visit the nearest police station.

items.title: Identity verification required for sponsored ads on social media
items.summary: MELAKA: All advertisers using sponsored advertisements on social media platforms will be required to verify their identities in a bid to curb online fraud, Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching said. She said the measure, which also targets digital gambling activities and the misuse of prominent individuals' identities, is among the new responsibilities under the Risk Mitigation Code (RMC). She said the code will come into force tomorrow under the Online Safety Act 2025 (ONSA) and will apply to social media platform providers classified as licensees, namely those with more than eight million users in Malaysia.

items.title: Odeon Cinema fire sparks wave of nostalgic recollections
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Long before the era of multiplexes, reclining seats and online bookings, the Odeon Cinema was a gathering place where generations of moviegoers came together for entertainment, family outings and cherished memories. Many recall a cinema experience far removed from today's modern standards, but rich in character and nostalgia. For social media user Cloud Go, the former cinema brings back memories of a simpler time when affordability mattered more than comfort.

items.title: Thousands join Wesak Day rites at temples nationwide
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: In a spirit of peace and gratitude, Wesak Day was celebrated across the country today, reflecting Malaysia's multi-racial and multi-religious society. He said the celebrations, which fall on a weekend, offer devotees a timely opportunity to attend religious ceremonies and activities organised at the temple. He said Wesak Day is observed to commemorate three significant events in the life of Gautama Buddha; his birth, enlightenment and passing.

items.title: Over 100,000 expected to throng Brickfields for Malaysia's largest Wesak Day celebration
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: More than 100,000 devotees, tourists and well-wishers are expected to throng the Buddhist Maha Vihara in Brickfields today as Malaysia's largest Wesak Day celebration gets under way with religious observances, charitable initiatives and a grand candlelight procession featuring 35 decorated floats. The temple's honorary secretary, Tilak Leslie, said preparations for the annual celebration began two months ago to ensure the smooth running of the religious and community activities planned for devotees. Leslie said the highlight of this year's celebration would be the Wesak candlelight procession, which will feature 35 floats representing the Malaysian, Nepali, Bangladeshi, Thai and Myanmar Buddhist communities.

items.title: Heavy rain and strong winds forecast for parts of Selangor, Perak, Pahang, and Johor till evening
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 31 — MetMalaysia has issued an urgent thunderstorm warning for parts of four states across peninsular Malaysia until 5pm today. Affected districts are Kinta, Kampar, Hilir Perak, Batang Padang and Muallim in Perak, Raub and Bentong in Pahang, Hulu Selangor in Selangor, and Mersing and Kota Tinggi in Johor. The warning is activated when MetMalaysia detects thunderstorms with rainfall intensity exceeding 20mm per hour that are expected to arrive within one hour.

items.title: Malaysia now requires identity checks for sponsored social media ads, says deputy comms minister
items.summary: MELAKA, May 31 — All advertisers using sponsored advertisements on social media platforms will be required to verify identities in a bid to curb online fraud, Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching said. She said the measure, which also targets digital gambling activities and the misuse of prominent individuals’ identities, is among the new responsibilities under the Risk Mitigation Code (RMC). She said the code will come into force today (June 1) under the Online Safety Act 2025 (ONSA) and will apply to social media platform providers classified as licensees, namely those with more than eight million users in Malaysia.

items.title: Laos cave rescue: Diving is last resort to find remaining miners, says Malaysian diver
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Critical details provided by five rescued survivors are guiding the search for two gold miners that have been trapped inside a flooded cave in central Laos for over a week. With conditions being hazardous, Malaysian technical cave diver Lee Kian Lie, who is part of the international rescue mission, said that diving is currently the last resort. "Everyone here is still highly motivated to look for the missing two based on the information given by the surviving five (miners).

items.title: Traffic slows on highways in peninsula as holiday volume exceeds two million vehicles
items.summary: Senior General Manager of Operations at PLUS Malaysia Berhad, Mohd Yusuf Abdul Aziz, said traffic on the North-South Expressway exceeded two million vehicles over the past two days, Berita Harian reported this afternoon. “For the Central Region, traffic is slow after exiting the Sungai Besi Toll Plaza towards the city centre, Gua Tempurung to Gopeng and Nilai to the Seremban Rest Area heading south. Traffic is also slow from Sedenak to Kulai and Senai to Kulai.

items.title: Khaled questions Oslo’s commitment to ties with Malaysia as missile refund hangs in balance
items.summary: SINGAPORE, May 31 — Malaysia is doubtful it will recover nearly RM543 million already paid to Norway for anti-ship missiles and has delivered a veiled warning that the cancellation could harm their long-standing bilateral relationship. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin raised the issue during a bilateral meeting with his Norwegian counterpart on the sidelines of the Shangri-La Dialogue here today, Bernama reported. Norway’s Defence Minister Tore O. Sandvik had earlier conveyed an apology to Malaysia over the export licence cancellation.

items.title: Five men arrested over armed brawl at flat in Port Klang
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 31 — Police have arrested five local men suspected of being involved in a brawl at Flat Seri Perantau in Port Klang Friday. Klang Selatan district police chief ACP Lim Jit Huey said the suspects, aged between 18 and 47, were detained after police received a report on the incident at 4pm Friday. “Acting on information, the Klang Selatan district police headquarters (IPD) arrested five local men.

items.title: US chemical spill death toll rises to 11
items.summary: LOS ANGELES: The death toll from a chemical spill at a paper plant in Washington state has risen to 11 as rescuers recovered the bodies of all nine missing people, US authorities said on Saturday. A huge tank holding tens of thousands of gallons of a highly caustic substance imploded on Tuesday at the plant in Longview, Washington state, sparking a major search operation. Rescuers said on Wednesday that two people had died while nine others remained missing.

items.title: Indonesia faces question of structural integrity versus short-term fixes
items.summary: QUESTIONS abound as the Indonesian rupiah reaches new depths. Even against the ringgit, the rupiah fell to a historic low of RM1 to 4,500 rupiah this week. Prabowo has been highly vocal recently, railing against both Indonesian conglomerates as well as foreign investors for profiting unfairly in exploiting the country's natural resources, be they minerals or its vast hectarage of oil palms.

items.title: MIC respects Perikatan’s revocation of entry approval, says Vigneswaran
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 31 — MIC said it respects Perikatan Nasional’s decision to revoke its earlier approval for the party to join the opposition coalition. According to Sinar Harian , MIC president Tan Sri SA Vigneswaran said the party had no issue with any PN component, including PAS. “When the offer was made previously, PN’s leadership was led by Bersatu and at that time MIC was indeed prepared to join it.

items.title: Wesak Day draws foreign visitors impressed by cultural, spiritual experience
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The sights, sounds and traditions of Wesak Day drew not only Buddhist devotees but also visitors from abroad, many of whom described the celebration as a unique cultural and spiritual experience. For Australian tourist Lean Mitchell, 32, who was visiting Malaysia for the first time, witnessing the annual celebration at a Buddhist temple left a lasting impression. Mitchell said she was particularly moved by the candle-lighting ceremony and the sight of devotees offering prayers and flowers.

items.title: Foreigner killed in Odeon building fire classified as sudden death case [WATCH]
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Police have classified the death of a 56-year-old foreign worker, whose body was found following a fire at the former Odeon Cinema building along Penang Road here last night, as a sudden death case. Northeast district deputy police chief Superintendent Lee Swee Sake said police received a report from the public about 8.50pm regarding the fire. Lee said investigations into the incident were ongoing.

items.title: B15 biodiesel rollout starts June 1: Why it matters for Malaysia’s energy security
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 31 — Malaysia is set to enter a new phase in its energy transition amid a global supply crisis, with the biodiesel blend rate in Peninsular Malaysia to be gradually raised from B10 to B15 starting tomorrow, June 1. The implementation will see B15 biodiesel comprising 15 per cent palm oil-based biodiesel, known as Palm Methyl Ester (PME), and 85 per cent petroleum diesel, compared with the existing B10 blend of 10 per cent PME, without requiring engine modifications for most diesel vehicles. He said that by-products from the palm oil industry, including sludge from crude palm oil production, have the potential to be used in biodiesel and aviation fuel production, creating a new value chain in the country’s commodity sector.

items.title: Sabah embarks on five-year initiative to document multi-ethnic heritage, says Hajiji
items.summary: Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said the documentation effort is important to ensure that ethnic heritage and traditions are not lost, while also strengthening the Kaamatan Festival as one of the main tourism products capable of elevating Sabah’s profile on the world stage. “Kaamatan Festival is not just a cultural celebration, but an important platform to preserve ethnic traditions, foster tolerance, and strengthen political stability and state development. Sabah has more than 30 ethnic groups.

items.title: PM calls for love, kindness and moderation in Wesak Day message
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 31 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has called on all Malaysians to reflect on the noble values that form the foundation of harmonious community life in conjunction with the Wesak Day celebration today. He said these values, particularly wisdom, moderation, compassion and kindness towards fellow human beings, are not only celebrated within religious traditions but remain highly relevant in building a society founded on mutual understanding and respect. In a nation characterised by diverse cultures, religions and backgrounds, he said the values of love, compassion and kindness highlighted during Wesak carry profound significance, especially at a time when certain groups seek to sow discord among different races and faiths.

items.title: KLIA Aerotrain returns to full 24-hour service from midnight, says transport minister
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 31 — The Aerotrain at KLIA Terminal 1 will resume full 24-hour operations starting 12am, June 1. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the service is now ready after months of rigorous safety upgrades and testing by Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd in coordination with the Land Public Transport Agency (Apad) and verified by independent railway assessors. The programme included detailed inspections, system testing, validation and extended trial runs under both passenger and non-passenger conditions.

items.title: Norway apologises but sticks to decision on Malaysia missile deal
items.summary: SINGAPORE, May 31 — Norwegian Defence Minister Tore O. Sandvik has apologised for his government’s decision to cancel the export licence for Naval Strike Missiles (NSM) to Malaysia, said Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin. He added that Sandvik also detailed the reasons behind Norway’s cancellation decision. The Norwegian government previously revoked Malaysia’s export licence for the NSM system and its launchers, citing national security concerns.

items.title: PAS info chief questions Bersatu strength, floats split for GE16
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 31 — Widening cracks within Perikatan Nasional (PN) were exposed today after PAS publicly questioned Bersatu’s electoral readiness and floated the possibility of going it alone at the next general election. The Islamist party’s information chief Ahmad Fadhli Shaari also delivered a pointed critique of Bersatu’s organisational strength and electoral performance, questioning whether continued cooperation remained viable. The Pasir Mas MP said that prolonged organisational instability would not amount to genuine strengthening of a party.

items.title: Putrajaya residents told not to panic over cannon salutes for King’s parade
items.summary: Putrajaya district police chief Aidi Sham Mohamed said the sounds would be from ceremonial cannon salutes by the Malaysian Armed Forces during the Trooping of the Colours Parade for His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, the King of Malaysia, at Dataran Pahlawan Negara in Presint 1, Putrajaya. He said two ceremonial cannon shots would be fired during the full rehearsal starting at 8.30am on Wednesday (June 3). “The actual ceremony on Friday (June 5) will feature 21 cannon shots, to be fired at the same time,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Eleven financial institutions ready for amended hire purchase rules from June 1, says Domestic Trade Ministry
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 31 — A total of 11 financial institutions and hire purchase companies are ready to offer hire purchase financing under the amended provisions of the Hire Purchase (Amendment) Act 2026, which comes into force on June 1, according to the Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN). Armizan said the ministry will officially enforce the Hire Purchase (Amendment) Act 2026 from June 1 as part of efforts to strengthen consumer protection and ensure a fairer, more transparent and competitive hire purchase financing system. In line with the amendments, the Hire Purchase (Term Charges) Regulations 2026 [P.U.

items.title: Gawai rush begins: Sarawak deputy minister urges safer journeys, wiser spending
items.summary: KAPIT, May 31 — Those returning to their hometowns and villagers for gawai Dayak must always prioritise safety and also be considerate to other road users. “Those travelling by boats must also practise the safety measures; the same goes for those boarding the express buses.” The Bukit Goram assemblyman also called upon those celebrating to spend prudently during this festive season, amidst the current global situation. Jamit also took the opportunity to wish fellow Dayaks a joyful and meaningful Gawai celebration.

items.title: Hajiji welcomes RM1.5b interim special grant for Sabah, wants swift disbursement
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 31 — Sabah Chief Minister Datuk Seri Panglima Hajiji Noor has welcomed the RM1.5 billion interim special grant for the state announced by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim yesterday. “It proves that the Federal Government under Anwar is a responsible government that walks the talk. “The state government will work closely with the federal government to ensure all the provisions under MA63, especially the 40 per cent special grant, are fulfilled,” he said.

items.title: After one term in Skudai, DAP’s Marina Ibrahim says political chapter has ended
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 31 — Skudai assemblyman Marina Ibrahim today announced her retirement from politics, saying she will not defend her seat at the next Johor state election or seek any other elected position. Marina was elected as the Skudai assemblyman in the March 2022 Johor state election. Marina said she remains committed to serving the public and helping the community outside of politics.

items.title: Kelantan farmers urged to stop letting cattle roam as road accident risks rise
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 31 — The rising cost of livestock farming is believed to be among the main factors behind the continued practice of allowing cattle to roam freely in Kelantan, posing safety risks to road users, especially on main roads at night. Despite efforts by authorities to curb the issue of stray animals, some farmers still opt for traditional methods, allowing their livestock to forage on their own in open grazing areas to reduce feed and enclosure-construction costs. Kelantan Artificial Inseminators Association chairman Harun Hazim Zakaria said the increase in prices of feed and cost of building sheds and fencing has led some farmers to take the easier route by releasing their animals without continuous supervision.

items.title: Govt spot checks uncover ageing paddy farmers renting land to foreigners
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 31 — The Agriculture Department has uncovered a growing trend of elderly and inactive paddy farmers informally leasing their land to foreigners, prompting calls for tighter monitoring of such arrangements. The practice was detected during random field inspections carried out after farmers and landowners received assistance from the Agriculture and Food Security Ministry, New Straits Times reported today. “The department has detected a trend where landowners, especially veterans or those no longer active in farming, rent out or lease their plots to foreigners informally.

items.title: Malaysia, Singapore among 17 countries backing new framework to protect underwater infrastructure
items.summary: SINGAPORE, May 31 — Malaysia, together with Singapore and 15 other countries, yesterday launched Guiding Principles for Underwater Infrastructure Defence Exchanges (GUIDE), a framework for the security of critical underwater infrastructure (CUI). Held on the sidelines of the 23rd Shangri-La Dialogue (SLD), the launch brings together countries with common interests in the security of CUI. Malaysia’s Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said GUIDE is a framework of shared principles that opens up space for closer cooperation between participating countries in efforts to strengthen the security of CUI, which is increasingly important to communication, trade, and global stability.

items.title: As Hamzah weighs next move, Mukhriz says no talks with Pejuang
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 31 — Pejuang president Datuk Seri Mukhriz Mahathir has dismissed speculation that former Bersatu deputy president Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin is heading to the party following his expulsion from Bersatu earlier this year. Mukhriz said reports suggesting Hamzah had applied to join Pejuang or was negotiating a political arrangement with the party were unfounded, New Straits Times r eported today. Among those reportedly associated with the group, informally known as “Team Reset”, are Indera Mahkota MP Datuk Saifuddin Abdullah, Machang MP Wan Ahmad Fayhsal Wan Ahmad Kamal and Tasek Gelugor MP Datuk Wan Saiful Wan Jan.

items.title: Singaporean hiker injured after fall while descending Gunung Datuk
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 31 — A Singaporean man was injured after allegedly falling at a rocky section while descending Gunung Datuk in Rembau near here this morning. Kota Fire and Rescue Station chief Senior Fire Officer II Asnizam Kamberon said the department received a call regarding the incident involving the 52-year-old victim at 10.29am. “The victim was among 11 hikers taking part in a climb to the summit of Gunung Datuk, accompanied by two Negeri Sembilan Forestry Mountain Guides (MGP).
